protocol.name: xfce_input_device_list_private_v1

interface.name: xfce_input_device_list_private_v1
interface.description:
This interface provides a global that will list input devices currently attached to the system, along with their current and possible settings.
This interface is informational and read-only: it reports each device's settings, their defaults, and the values the device supports, but provides no means to change them. Settings are configured through other Xfce mechanisms; this protocol reflects the resulting state back to clients.
It is intended to be private to the Xfce desktop implementation, and should not be used by other applications. This protocol may introduce incompatible changes at any time, without warning, and without following normal, accepted guidelines about Wayland protocol versioning.

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy the binding to the global

request.description: Destroys the client's binding to the global. Existing device objects are unaffected and remain valid until released.

event.name: device
event.description:
Notifies the client of an input device currently attached to the system. This event is sent for every device present when the global is bound, and again whenever a device is later added.
The device's name and capabilities are immutable for the lifetime of the device and are provided here. Following this event, the compositor sends the device's applicable settings on the newly created object before sending the next done event.
A single physical device may expose more than one capability; it is always represented by exactly one xfce_input_device_v1 object regardless of how many capabilities it has.

event.name: done

description.summary: a batch of newly added devices has been fully sent

event.description:
Sent after the compositor has finished emitting a group of newly added devices, including each new device's full set of settings. It is sent once after the initial set of devices present when the global was bound, and again after each later group of devices is added.
This event is not sent when the settings of an existing device change; such updates are delivered as standalone setting events on the affected device object.

enum.name: capabilities
enum.description: The kinds of input a device is able to produce. A device may have more than one of these set. The values mirror libinput's device capabilities.

entry.name: pointer
entry.summary: device produces relative pointer events (mouse, touchpad, trackpoint)

entry.name: tablet_tool
entry.summary: device is a graphics tablet driven by a tool (pen)

entry.name: tablet_pad
entry.summary: device is the button pad of a graphics tablet

interface.name: xfce_input_device_v1
interface.description:
Represents a single input device, and exposes the settings the compositor is able to apply to it. The device's name and capabilities are immutable for the device's lifetime and are provided as arguments of the xfce_input_device_list_private_v1.device event that creates this object.
Immediately after this object is created, the compositor sends one event for each setting that is applicable to the device. A setting event is only sent when the setting is available on the device; the absence of a setting event means the device does not support that setting at all. Where a setting is a choice among several values, the event also reports which of those values the device supports.
Whenever a setting changes for any reason (for example, the compositor applying a new configuration), the corresponding event is sent again with an updated current value.

request.name: release
request.description: Tells the compositor the client will no longer use this object. The client should send this after receiving the removed event, but may send it at any time if it is no longer interested in the device.

event.name: removed

description.summary: device has been removed from the system

event.description: The device has been removed from the system. No further events will be sent on this object; the client should release it.

event.name: send_events

description.summary: how the device may be enabled or disabled

event.description: Reports the device's send-events mode, which controls whether and when the device generates input events. This setting is available on every device.

event.name: accel_speed
event.description: Reports the device's normalized pointer-acceleration speed, in the range [-1, 1], where 0 is the device's default acceleration, -1 is the slowest, and 1 is the fastest. Only sent for devices that use pointer acceleration.

event.name: accel_profile
event.description: Reports the device's pointer-acceleration profile, which selects the algorithm used to map device movement to pointer movement. Only sent for devices that support more than the "none" profile.

event.name: natural_scroll
event.description: Reports whether natural scrolling is enabled, in which the content follows the finger or wheel direction rather than the scroll bar. Only sent for devices that support natural scrolling.

event.name: scroll_method
event.description: Reports the method used to turn device movement into scroll events. Only sent for devices that support a configurable scroll method.

event.name: scroll_button
event.description: Reports the button that, while held, turns device movement into scroll events when the on_button_down scroll method is in use. The value is an evdev button code, or 0 if button scrolling is disabled. Only sent for devices that support the on_button_down scroll method.

arg.name: default_value
arg.summary: default button code, or 0 if disabled

arg.name: current_value
arg.summary: current button code, or 0 if disabled

event.name: scroll_button_lock
event.description: Reports whether the scroll button latches: when enabled, the button is pressed and released once to start and stop scrolling rather than being held down. Only sent for devices that support the on_button_down scroll method.

event.name: click_method
event.description: Reports the method used to determine which button a click maps to on a clickpad, either by the area of the pad pressed or by the number of fingers down. Only sent for devices that support a configurable click method.

event.name: clickfinger_button_map

description.summary: finger-count to button mapping for clickfinger

event.description: Reports how a one-, two-, or three-finger click maps to a logical button when the clickfinger click method is in use. Only sent for devices that support the clickfinger click method.

event.name: left_handed
event.description: Reports whether the device's buttons are configured for left-handed use, which typically swaps the left and right buttons. Only sent for devices that support a left-handed layout.

event.name: middle_emulation
event.description: Reports whether middle-button emulation is enabled, in which a simultaneous left and right button press is reported as a middle button press. Only sent for devices that support middle-button emulation.

event.name: tap
event.description: Reports whether tap-to-click is enabled, in which a tap on the device is reported as a button click. Only sent for devices that support tap-to-click.

event.name: tap_finger_count

description.summary: number of fingers usable for tapping

event.description: Reports the number of fingers that can be detected for tap-to-click on the device. This is a fixed property of the device and cannot be changed; it is provided so the client knows which tap-related settings are meaningful. Only sent for devices that support tap-to-click.

event.name: tap_button_map

description.summary: finger-count to button mapping for tapping

event.description: Reports how a one-, two-, or three-finger tap maps to a logical button. Only sent for devices that support tap-to-click.

event.name: tap_drag
event.description: Reports whether tap-and-drag is enabled, in which a tap followed by a finger movement drags with the left button held. Only sent for devices that support tap-to-click.

event.name: tap_drag_lock
event.description: Reports whether drag lock is enabled for tap-and-drag, in which lifting the finger does not immediately end the drag. Only sent for devices that support tap-to-click.

event.name: three_finger_drag
event.description:
Reports whether dragging with three (or four) fingers is enabled, in which placing the corresponding number of fingers down and moving drags with the left button held. Only sent for devices that support three-finger drag.
The max_fingers argument reports the largest number of fingers the device supports for this gesture: if it is 3, only the enabled_3fg value is usable; if it is 4, both enabled_3fg and enabled_4fg are usable.

event.name: dwt
event.description: Reports whether disable-while-typing is enabled, in which the device is temporarily disabled while a keyboard is being used to avoid spurious input. Only sent for devices that support disable-while-typing.

event.name: dwt_timeout
event.description: Reports the time, in milliseconds, between the last key event and the device being re-enabled when disable-while-typing is active. Only sent for devices that support a configurable disable-while-typing timeout.

event.name: dwtp
event.description: Reports whether disable-while-trackpointing is enabled, in which the device is temporarily disabled while a trackpoint is being used. Only sent for devices that support disable-while-trackpointing.

event.name: dwtp_timeout
event.description: Reports the time, in milliseconds, between the last trackpoint event and the device being re-enabled when disable-while-trackpointing is active. Only sent for devices that support a configurable disable-while-trackpointing timeout.

event.name: rotation
event.description: Reports the clockwise rotation, in whole degrees in the range [0, 360), applied to all motion events from the device. Only sent for devices that support rotation.

event.name: calibration
event.description:
Reports the calibration matrix applied to the device's absolute coordinates, as the six values [a, b, c, d, e, f] forming the first two rows of the 3x3 transformation matrix
[ a b c ] [ d e f ] [ 0 0 1 ]
applied to normalized device coordinates. The identity matrix (a=1, e=1, all others 0) represents no calibration. Only sent for devices that can be calibrated.

event.name: area

description.summary: active region of the tablet surface

event.description: Reports the rectangular sub-region of a graphics tablet's surface that is active; tablet-tool input is scaled to this region as if it were the full surface. The region is given as the upper-left point (x1, y1) and the lower-right point (x2, y2), each normalized to the range [0, 1] as a proportion of the device's width and height, so the full surface is (0, 0) to (1, 1). The conditions x1 is less than x2 and y1 is less than y2 always hold. Only sent for devices that support configuring an active area.

arg.name: default_x1
arg.summary: default upper-left x, in the range [0, 1]

arg.name: default_y1
arg.summary: default upper-left y, in the range [0, 1]

arg.name: default_x2
arg.summary: default lower-right x, in the range [0, 1]

arg.name: default_y2
arg.summary: default lower-right y, in the range [0, 1]

arg.name: current_x1
arg.summary: current upper-left x, in the range [0, 1]

arg.name: current_y1
arg.summary: current upper-left y, in the range [0, 1]

arg.name: current_x2
arg.summary: current lower-right x, in the range [0, 1]

arg.name: current_y2
arg.summary: current lower-right y, in the range [0, 1]

enum.name: natural_scroll_state
enum.description: Whether the content follows the finger or wheel direction.

enum.name: left_handed_state
enum.description: Whether the device's buttons are configured for left-handed use.

enum.name: send_events_modes
enum.description: Controls whether a device generates input events. The supported argument of the send_events event is a bitfield of these values; enabled is implicit and always available. The default and current arguments hold a single value.

entry.name: disabled_on_external_mouse
entry.summary: the device is disabled while an external mouse is attached

enum.name: accel_profiles
enum.description: The acceleration algorithms a device may use. The supported argument of the accel_profile event is a bitfield of these values; the default and current arguments hold a single value.

entry.name: flat
entry.summary: movement is scaled by a constant factor with no acceleration

entry.name: adaptive
entry.summary: movement is accelerated based on the device's speed

entry.name: custom
entry.summary: movement is mapped by a user-defined acceleration function

enum.name: scroll_methods
enum.description: The methods by which device movement may be turned into scroll events. The supported argument of the scroll_method event is a bitfield of these values; the default and current arguments hold a single value.

entry.name: two_finger
entry.summary: scrolling occurs when two fingers are down

entry.name: edge
entry.summary: scrolling occurs along the bottom or right edge

entry.name: on_button_down
entry.summary: scrolling occurs while the scroll button is held

enum.name: scroll_button_lock_state
enum.description: Whether the scroll button must be held or latches with a press and release.

entry.name: disabled
entry.summary: the scroll button must be held down to scroll

entry.name: enabled
entry.summary: the scroll button latches on and off

enum.name: click_methods

description.summary: ways button clicks are produced on a clickpad

enum.description: The methods by which a clickpad determines which button a click maps to. The supported argument of the click_method event is a bitfield of these values; the default and current arguments hold a single value.

entry.name: button_areas
entry.summary: the button is chosen by the region of the pad that is pressed

entry.name: clickfinger
entry.summary: the button is chosen by the number of fingers down

enum.name: button_map
enum.description: How a one-, two-, or three-finger tap or click maps to logical buttons. Used by both the tap_button_map and clickfinger_button_map events.

enum.name: middle_emulation_state
enum.description: Whether a simultaneous left and right button press is reported as a middle button press.

enum.name: tap_state
enum.description: Whether a tap on the device is reported as a button click.

enum.name: drag_state
enum.description: Whether a tap followed by finger movement drags with the button held.

enum.name: drag_lock_state
enum.description: Whether lifting the finger immediately ends a tap-and-drag, and if not, how the drag is eventually ended.

entry.name: disabled
entry.summary: lifting the finger ends the drag immediately

entry.name: enabled_timeout
entry.summary: the drag continues until a timeout expires after the finger is lifted

entry.name: enabled_sticky
entry.summary: the drag continues until the finger is tapped again

enum.name: three_finger_drag_state
enum.description: Whether placing three or four fingers down and moving drags with the button held.

entry.name: enabled_3fg
entry.summary: dragging is started with three fingers

entry.name: enabled_4fg
entry.summary: dragging is started with four fingers

enum.name: dwt_state
enum.description: Whether the device is temporarily disabled while a keyboard is in use.

enum.name: dwtp_state
enum.description: Whether the device is temporarily disabled while a trackpoint is in use.
